subject: Anthropology
title: Totemism
description: A micro-lesson that makes Totemism usable.

hook.text: The eagle clan cannot eat eagles. The bear clan cannot hunt bears. Each group is bound to its animal.

buildup.text: Totemism links human social groups to natural species or objects, creating a sacred bond between society and nature.

discovery.text: Durkheim saw totems as symbols of the group itself. Worshipping the totem meant worshipping society.

twist.text: Lévi-Strauss disagreed. Totems aren't worshipped — they're used to think with, classifying the social world.

climax.text: Totemism shows how humans use nature to organize society — mapping the wild world onto the social one.

punchline.text: Nature provides the symbols; culture gives them meaning.

quiz.question: How did Lévi-Strauss interpret totemism?
options: As a system for classifying the social world
